content:
Design Doc: Identity-Based SASE Administrative Fabric

Status: Operational. Scope: administrative access to sensitive cloud infrastructure using an Authenticate-then-Connect ZTNA fabric.

1. Why (Engineering Problem Statement)

Legacy 'Castle-and-Moat' VPNs grant excessive lateral trust once a perimeter is breached. Administrative access must enforce 'Verify Explicitly' and 'Least Privilege' across cloud boundaries.

2. Authenticate-then-Connect

WireGuard establishes a stateless transport handshake, then a separate out-of-band authorization gate (Active Directory/LDAP) validates whether the peer is allowed to connect. Only authorized peers obtain tunnel access for the least-privilege target set.

AUTHENTICATE-THEN-CONNECT

Client --(WireGuard UDP handshake)--> Fabric
        → No data-plane trust until
          Peer Authorization via AD/LDAP succeeds

Authorize(peer_dn, admin_policy) -> Allow|Deny
Deny immediately on AD disablement

3. Networking Spec (Hub-and-Spoke)

The VPC fabric is implemented with a hub-and-spoke topology: a centralized routing/peering hub connects spoke VPCs that host management targets. This isolates blast radius while enabling controlled private connectivity.

           [Spoke VPC A]
                |
        (Private Peering)
                |
   [Spoke VPC B] -- HUB VPC -- [Admin Gate]
                |
                |
        (Policy-Scope Connectivity)

MSS Clamping Technical Note: Enforced TCPMSS --set-mss 1280 at the iptables level to prevent packet fragmentation across 1500-MTU cloud provider boundaries.

4. Security Posture (NIST 800-207)

Mapping to NIST 800-207: dynamic policy enforcement, continuous authorization semantics, and micro-segmentation for administratively privileged flows.

Bastion Layer Removal

Removed the classic bastion trust pivot. Administrative access no longer relies on a single broadly trusted jump host.

Micro-segmentation via AWS Security Groups

Implemented least-privilege reachability using AWS Security Groups to constrain Admin Peer access. Connectivity to DB targets is routed through DB-Proxy permissions, explicitly blocking DB-Master direct reachability.

5. Operational Reliability

Tunnel stability is enforced via MSS clamping and controlled transport behavior across peered VPC boundaries. Access revocation is applied immediately when an AD account is disabled, preventing stale authorization windows.

6. Audit & Governance

All tunnel authorization decisions and peer-to-peer transfer events are emitted to centralized logging for auditability and incident response workflows.
